properties.displayName: Brute Force — Repeated Failed Logons from Single Source (T1110)
properties.description: Detects five or more failed logon attempts (EventID 4625) from the same source IP within a 10-minute window. Critical severity — credential access is the inflection point for most attack chains. When this alert fires, immediately check whether any failure was followed by a successful logon (EventID 4624) from the same IP. If yes, escalate to P1. The query excludes loopback, link-local addresses, and machine accounts to reduce noise. Tune the failure threshold against your environment's baseline.
properties.query:
let FailureThreshold = 5;
let TimeWindow = 10m;
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4625
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where AccountType == 'User'
| where IpAddress !in ('127.0.0.1', '::1', '-', '')
| where IpAddress !startswith '169.254'
| summarize
    FailureCount    = count(),
    TargetAccounts  = make_set(TargetUserName, 20),
    FirstAttempt    = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt     = max(TimeGenerated),
    Computer        = any(Computer)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, TimeWindow)
| where FailureCount >= FailureThreshold
| extend AttackDurationSeconds = datetime_diff('second', LastAttempt, FirstAttempt)
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    IpAddress,
    FailureCount,
    TargetAccounts,
    FirstAttempt,
    LastAttempt,
    AttackDurationSeconds,
    Computer
| order by FailureCount desc

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat: {{FailureCount}} failed logon attempts from {{IpAddress}} between {{FirstAttempt}} and {{LastAttempt}}. Targeted accounts: {{TargetAccounts}}
